mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling federal funds rates indicate accommodative monetary policy successfully stimulating consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS increases and FEDFUNDS decreases, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy in more than 60% of post-recession quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside falling industrial production signal a synchronized contraction in investment and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined decline in HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict negative returns, with positive forward returns occurring in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during expansion phases, or the signal produces negative predictive accuracy in more than 55% of quarters.
